_comment: Checked-in baseline for `node tools/check-test-results.mjs` (bun run test:gated). `failures` is an array of entry objects, one per exact full test name (format: 'describe > test', or bare 'test' when there is no describe block) that is allowed to fail without gating a commit — see AGENTS.md's 'judge by the set of failing test names' policy. Each entry carries: `name` (the exact test name), `kind` ('env' = blocked on something this environment lacks; 'bug' = a real product defect parked here so it doesn't block unrelated work — the checker warns when a bug entry ages past 30 days, because bugs are open work, not accepted behavior), `addedAt` (ISO date the entry entered this list), and `notes` (what it's blocked on). Do not add entries here to silence a new regression; only pre-existing failures belong here. If an entry starts passing, tools/check-test-results.mjs reports it under 'known-failing now passing' so a human can trim it deliberately.

failures.name: runtime resume plan > harness run input resolves Vertex project and global location from config
failures.notes: Requires a resolvable Vertex project/location (gcloud config or repo-root .ge.json) that this environment doesn't have configured. Same root cause as the harness-runner.test.mjs entry below — 'antigravity-sdk harness unavailable in this environment' per AGENTS.md.

failures.name: harness runner config > resolves Vertex project from repo-root .ge.json (via c12)
failures.notes: Same as above: expects __test.resolveVertexDefaults() to resolve a truthy Vertex project from ambient gcloud/.ge.json config, which this environment doesn't have.

failures.name: multi-agent emitter > multi-step spec → SequentialAgent + sub-agents + _pick, tools.py unchanged
failures.notes: generateAgent() shells out to `factory.mjs from-usecase`, which fails with "Cannot find module '/home/user/ge-agent-factory/src/use-cases.js'" — a generated use-case catalog file that isn't materialized in this checkout/environment. Not a code bug in the emitter itself.

failures.name: multi-agent emitter > single-step spec → single Agent + tools=source_adapters, no multi-agent constructs
failures.notes: Same generateAgent()/from-usecase catalog-module failure as the multi-step case above.

failures.name: multi-agent emitter > BUG1: every sub-agent instruction carries objective + outOfScope + refusal governance
failures.notes: Same generateAgent()/from-usecase catalog-module failure as the multi-step case above.

failures.name: multi-agent emitter > BUG2: before_agent_callback is on every sub-agent
failures.notes: Same generateAgent()/from-usecase catalog-module failure as the multi-step case above.

failures.name: multi-agent emitter > BUG3: sub-agents have distinct output_key values
failures.notes: Same generateAgent()/from-usecase catalog-module failure as the multi-step case above.

failures.name: round-trip recovers tool names, systems, and workflow step order
failures.notes: Genuine assertion mismatch, not an environment issue: expects the recovered step id 'balance__document__pull' (double underscore) but gets 'balance_document_pull' (single underscore) — an OKF round-trip normalization discrepancy in spec-to-okf.mjs. Tracked as a real bug to fix separately; kept in the known list so it doesn't block unrelated work.

failures.name: capability spine: queries/, tests/, documents/ concepts emitted + round-trip
failures.notes: Same spec-to-okf.mjs round-trip normalization discrepancy as above (same file, same underlying id-mangling behavior).

failures.name: synthesizeSystem returns a live system for an NL description (offline)
failures.notes: Calls the real synthesis CLI, which imports the Python `simulator_runtime` module — not installed in this environment (matches AGENTS.md's 'missing Python simulator_runtime module' known failure).

failures.name: POST /api/systems/synthesize returns a system via the router (offline)
failures.notes: Same missing Python `simulator_runtime` module dependency as the synthesizeSystem entry above; the route call fails upstream in the same synthesis CLI.
